description: Arizona Water Company operational schema: customers, water systems, tiered rates, monthly usage/billing, water-quality reports, and account alerts. Contains PII and regulated water-quality data.

Arizona Water Company customer master file across 27 communities in 8 Arizona counties. Holds residential, commercial, agricultural and industrial accounts. Contains direct PII (names, service and billing addresses, email, phone).
Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each customer account.
Unique AWC account number in format AWC-[City Code]-[Sequential]. Primary billing/service identifier. Confidential PII.
Full name of the customer (person or organisation). Direct PII.
Street address where water service is delivered. Direct PII / location.
City of the service location. Quasi-identifier.
Arizona county of the service location (one of 8 served counties).
ZIP code of the service location. Quasi-identifier.
Customer classification: Residential, Commercial, Industrial, Agriculture. Drives rate structure and conservation programs.
AWC water system serving this customer (e.g. Pinal Valley, Sedona). Determines applicable rates and water quality.
Customer email address. Direct PII and a marketing-contact channel.
Customer telephone number. Direct PII.
Account state: Active, Suspended, Closed, Pending.
Name on the billing account; may differ from the service customer. PII.
Mailing address for billing. Direct PII / location.
City portion of the billing address.
ZIP code portion of the billing address.
Date water service began for the account.
Timestamp of the most recent change to the customer record.
GDPR/CCPA marketing-consent flag. TRUE = opted OUT and must not receive marketing email. Critical compliance control.
AWC's 24 water systems serving 27 Arizona communities. Each system has a distinct water source, rate structure and conservation focus. Operational reference data, largely public.
Surrogate primary key identifying each water system.
Unique water system name (e.g. Pinal Valley System).
Communities served by this system.
Primary Arizona county served by this system.
Water system type: Groundwater, Surface Water, or Mixed.
Water source description (Wells, Colorado River, Local Rivers, Mixed). Drives supply vulnerability.
Primary physical water source for the system.
Count of customers served by the system.
Estimated population served by the system.
Total annual water usage for the system, in acre-feet.
AWC conservation priorities for this system.
Date of the most recent regulatory compliance check.
Operational status of the system (e.g. Active).
Rated/permitted annual system capacity in acre-feet. Compare against total_annual_usage_acre_feet for utilization.
Conservation-oriented tiered rate structure. Tier 1 (essential use) is the lowest rate; Tier 4 (discretionary use) the highest. Rates vary by system, customer type and year. Public rate schedule.
Surrogate primary key for the rate row.
Foreign key to water_systems; the system this rate applies to.
Rate year/period (e.g. 2026).
Customer class the rate applies to: Residential, Commercial, Agricultural.
Fixed monthly base charge before usage tiers.
Lower bound (gallons) of Tier 1 usage band.
Upper bound (gallons) of Tier 1 usage band (essential use).
Tier 1 rate per 1,000 gallons (lifeline/essential, lowest).
Lower bound (gallons) of Tier 2 usage band.
Upper bound (gallons) of Tier 2 usage band.
Tier 2 rate per 1,000 gallons (moderate use).
Lower bound (gallons) of Tier 3 usage band.
Upper bound (gallons) of Tier 3 usage band.
Tier 3 rate per 1,000 gallons (higher use).
Lower bound (gallons) of Tier 4 usage band.
Upper bound (gallons) of Tier 4 usage band.
Tier 4 rate per 1,000 gallons (discretionary, highest).
Wastewater charge applied where applicable.
Date the rate row becomes effective.
Status of the rate row (e.g. Active).
Representative published tiered rate (Tier 1 lifeline rate per 1,000 gallons). The full ladder remains in tier1..tier4_rate_per_1000gal.

Customer monthly water usage and billing records. Tracks usage by tier and the charges derived from the conservation rate structure. Confidential for payment processing; combines consumption behaviour and financial data.
Surrogate primary key for the monthly usage/billing record.
Foreign key to customers; the account billed.
Foreign key to water_systems; the serving system.
First day of the billing month for this record.
Total water used by the customer in the billing month. Consumption behaviour.
Gallons billed in Tier 1 for the month.
Gallons billed in Tier 2 for the month.
Gallons billed in Tier 3 for the month.
Gallons billed in Tier 4 for the month.
Fixed base charge applied this month.
Charge for Tier 1 usage this month.
Charge for Tier 2 usage this month.
Charge for Tier 3 usage this month.
Charge for Tier 4 usage this month.
Wastewater charge applied this month.
Total charges before tax.
Tax applied to the bill.
Total amount due for the billing month.
Payment due date for the bill.
Billing payment status: Paid, Unpaid, Overdue, Disputed. Triggers collection workflows.
Date payment was received, if any. Behavioural/financial.
Amount paid against the bill.
Date the usage/billing record was created.
Cumulative meter register reading in gallons at the end of the billing month. Month usage = current reading minus prior reading.
Service meter identifier for the customer connection (format MTR-NNNNNN). Stable across billing months.

Monthly water-quality monitoring for all AWC systems, tested under the EPA Safe Drinking Water Act and published in Consumer Confidence Reports. Regulated readings; critical for public health and compliance.
Surrogate primary key for the quality report.
Foreign key to water_systems; the system tested.
Month the quality report covers.
Measured pH level of the water sample.
Turbidity in NTU (cloudiness). Regulated quality metric.
Residual chlorine concentration.
Whether bacteria were detected in the sample.
Lead concentration in parts per billion. EPA-regulated contaminant.
Copper concentration in parts per million. EPA-regulated contaminant.
Water hardness in parts per million.
Total dissolved solids in parts per million.
Overall quality rating: Excellent, Good, Fair, Poor.
Regulatory compliance status: Compliant, Warning, Violation. Critical for reporting.
Free-text notes on the system's quality for the period.
Date the quality report record was created.
Summary contaminant level derived from the regulated readings and compliance status: Low, Moderate, Elevated, High.

Service alerts and flags for customer accounts (payment overdue, high usage, low pressure, quality issue, leak suspected, conservation notice). Routes to different operational and compliance workflows.
Surrogate primary key for the alert.
Foreign key to customers; the account the alert concerns.
Foreign key to water_systems; the system involved.
Type of alert: High Usage, Payment Overdue, Low Pressure, Quality Issue, Leak Suspected, Conservation Notice, Service Suspension.
Alert severity: Critical, High, Medium, Low.
Date the alert was raised.
Free-text alert detail; may reference account balances or usage figures.
Recommended follow-up action for the alert.
Alert workflow status: Open, Acknowledged, Resolved.
Date the alert was resolved, if applicable.
Date the alert record was created.
